Echoes of Cilivisations

The Métiers d'Art Tribute to Great Civilisations series invites a contemplation of ancient worlds through four distinct dials.

Pharaonic Egypt is represented by the Bust of Akhenaten, sculpted in glyptique on Egyptian limestone, framed by turquoise and champlevé stone friezes. The Assyrian Empire's Lamassu of Sargon II emerges from Italian limestone, complemented by champlevé stone and flinqué enamel. Ancient Greece's Athena of Velletri, rendered in Thasos marble glyptique, is surrounded by a black enamel frieze and stone marquetry. Finally, imperial Rome's Tiber of the Iseum Campense, also in Italian marble, is paired with a micro-mosaic and gold leaf textured base.

Each timepiece, a limited edition of 15 pieces, meticulously reflects the decorative arts and historical authenticity of its era.

#VacheronConstantin #OneOfNotMany

Craft As A Tribute To Great Civilisations

The Maison's profound dedication to artistry is revealed in the creation of the Métiers d'Art Tribute to Great Civilisations series.

In close collaboration with curators of @museelouvre, Vacheron Constantin's artisans embarked on a three-year journey to translate monumental masterpieces into miniature works of art. Glyptique on stone, a first for the Maison, brings the central effigies to life, while micro-mosaic, engraving, and enamelling meticulously form the intricate friezes.

Each dial requires between 120 and 220 hours of work, a testament to the relentless quest for precision and excellence of the Maison.

#VacheronConstantin #OneOfNotMany

Each of our watches has its own story. An idea that inspired us to create it. A tradition that we wanted to revive. Or an almost impossible challenge that was successfully overcome. With #AspectsOfTime, we want to shed light on one of these hidden aspects once a month - today: sapphire crystal.

Sapphire crystal not only provides maximum clarity, it also acts as a sort of protective shield for a watch. 

This synthetic crystal, made of aluminium oxide, is almost as hard as a diamond and offers optimum protection for dials and displays. The “silent hero” of the watch industry is a hallmark of high-quality watches and is used in all Glashütte Original models – not only on the front, but on the case back of most timepieces as well. 

For some time now, our 39.5 mm SeaQ has also been among the models featuring a sapphire crystal case back: The classic three-hand model was originally – like its historical predecessor from 1969 – available exclusively with a stainless steel case back. This diving watch is now also available with a sapphire crystal case back, offering a glimpse into the refined mechanics. 

Yet, regardless of which side of your Glashütte Original features this coveted material, ensuring both transparency and protection: its qualities are crystal clear.

#GlashütteOriginal #GlashuetteOriginal #LuxuryWatches #SapphirCrystalBack

Should you find yourself within the walls of this Florentine apartment, whose very being is a Renaissance name-dropper’s dream, there will be much to steal your gaze.⁣
⁣
Within the private 2,476-square-foot residence in Palazzo Uguccioni—built for one Giovanni Uguccioni in the mid-1500s and most likely designed by Raphael, though Michelangelo and Palladio are also in the mix as possible architects—intricately carved wood paneling, richly textured wallpapers in regal reds and golds, and stone figurines on plinths lend the rooms gallery-like grandeur.⁣
⁣
But beyond all that, there is one feature that sums up the majesty of this apartment. Two red wingback chairs—custom-made by Gucci—stand poised on a platform. Before them, a pair of glazed doors have been flung open to reveal a front-seat view of the Gothic turrets and arches of the Palazzo Vecchio.⁣
⁣
Tap the link in our bio to read more. ⁣

In the early morning hours of April 18, 1906, a massive earthquake along the San Andreas Fault struck San Francisco, triggering fires that raged for days and ultimately caused far more destruction than the initial shaking. The disaster killed more than 3,000 people, destroyed over 28,000 buildings with flames sweeping across hundreds of city blocks and leveling much of the city. In the aftermath of the earthquake, an estimated 75,000 citizens simply left San Francisco. The remaining homeless population of 250,000 established makeshift camps. #HISTORY

Subtle Contrasts, Distinctive Signatures

The Overseas Dual Time Cardinal Points series reveals its character in the finer details.

First, a dash of contrast is introduced by the orange hands, precisely indicating the second time zone and the AM/PM function. Second, the matte anthracite grey finish on the bezel, crown, and pusher ring subtly enhances the titanium case, highlighting the cutaway notches that echo the Maison's Maltese Cross. Third, the dial itself presents a play of textures, from its grained center to the circular satin-finished inner minutes track and lacquered outer seconds track. Finally, complementing these elements, the rubber straps feature distinctive orange saddle-stitch details.

Each subtle contrast and each hidden detail contribute to the distinctive aesthetic of the Overseas Dual Time Cardinal Points models.

The new RM 55-01 Manual Winding is available in three versions : Carbon TPT®, White Quartz TPT® and Grey Quartz TPT®. These technical composites, used for the bezels, offer excellent shock and scratch resistance while reducing the overall weight of the watch. The materials’ unfinished damascene effect harmonises with the taut, clean lines of the three-part tonneau case. The calibre is securely anchored by four spline screws in microblasted titanium, framed with satin-finished pillars and polished bezels. The microblasted and rhodium-plated hands glide above a complex four-part flange that weaves together titanium, carbon fibre, and steel in a play of texture and relief.

The new RM 55-01 calibre, the RMUL4, is built on a grade 5 titanium baseplate., an alloy borrowed from the aerospace industry and highly prized at Richard Mille. To enhance its aesthetics, the baseplate is microblasted, sanded and treated with a black PVD coating. The going train is affixed by three bridges also produced from grade 5 titanium sanded and treated with Titalyt®. Nonetheless, working with this material remains a constant challenge. Milling grade 5 titanium is a herculean task: its extreme hardness and the brand’s low tolerance for error take a toll on every tool. The same uncompromising standards hold for the slightly thicker satin-brushed parts that structure the movement and highlight a tension between straight lines and curves.

🎥 @milledollars

Within the heart of Vacheron Constantin, a singular creation emerges: the Les Cabinotiers Minute Repeater Tourbillon Skeleton. This unique piece invites a discerning eye to explore its depths, where the movement Calibre 2755 TMR SQ reveals the timepiece's intricate architecture. One year of development was dedicated to the transformation of this movement, meticulously skeletonised to remove 40% of its main plate, yet preserving its inherent strength. 

The 473 components of the movement are adorned with nine distinct finishes: brushing flanks drawing, surface straightening, bevelling of the bridges, sandblasting of the bridges, rounding off of the tourbillon bar, mirror polishing of the hammers, circular graining, circling of the wheels, and sunburst finishing on the crown wheel core. This is not merely a watch; it is an intimate revelation of horological artistry.

#VacheronConstantin #OneOfNotMany

If Claude keeps giving you inconsistent results, your setup is the problem, not Claude.

Here's what separates a messy Claude Code setup from a high-performing one:

▪️ Don't leave CLAUDE.md empty or vague 
Do define your tech stack, architecture, and conventions clearly, it's the first thing Claude reads every session

▪️ Don't mix personal preferences into shared project files 
Do use CLAUDE.local.md for individual overrides so your workflow doesn't break your teammates'

▪️ Don't re-explain your tooling every session 
Do configure mcp.json once, GitHub, JIRA, Slack, databases all connected and version-controlled

▪️ Don't write one giant instruction file for everything 
Do use .claude/rules/ to separate concerns, code style, testing standards, API conventions load contextually

▪️ Don't repeat the same prompts for recurring workflows 
Do create .claude/commands/ slash commands, one keystroke runs your entire review or deploy process

▪️ Don't let Claude operate without guardrails 
Do set up .claude/hooks/ to validate, lint, and block unsafe operations automatically

▪️ Don't overload a single conversation with every task 
Do delegate to .claude/agents/ specialized sub-agents with isolated context for code review, security, and more

Your Claude Code setup is either working for your team or against it. Structure it once. Benefit from it forever.

What's one thing you'd add to this list?

On average, founders raise $1M+ post-accelerator.

What could that look like for you?

The way we do business is shifting, and in the coming months and years, entrepreneurs will have to sell their services and products to an AI agent before they ever reach a human.

Buyers are increasingly relying on AI tools to evaluate their options and run comparative product analyses. Therefore, ensuring your business appears favorably in these AI-generated comparisons is becoming a major strategic priority.

s it easier to renovate a house or ask people to move into a brand-new one with unfamiliar rooms, rules, and routines? With AI adoption, you want to take the renovation approach. It’s a blunder to try a big-bang approach to roll AI into an organization.

Always start with incremental changes to existing workflows and software. Remember that your teams already use dozens of software tools. These are the best starting points where leaders can inject AI and gently nudge user adoption.

For example, most front-line teams already live inside software, such as billing systems, customer relationship management systems, dispatch tools, maintenance software, or safety logs. Some of these systems may be clunky, but they are heavily used and largely unavoidable. The pain points within these systems could act as perfect entry points to introducing AI — places where users could see the value and welcome the initiative with open arms. When AI meets people where they already work, curiosity replaces resistance.

Take the case of fleet maintenance. Most technicians and supervisors already spend their days inside a computerized maintenance management system. Work orders are logged there. Inspections are recorded there. Breakdowns are investigated there.

An effective approach to introducing AI that can predict vehicle failures, for example, is to embed AI directly into the maintenance systems users already trust. AI can flag recurring fault codes, highlight assets with rising failure risk, or suggest prioritizing certain work orders before a breakdown occurs.

Happy World Quantum Day ⚛️ Today’s #GoogleDoodle features the Bloch sphere, a visualization of how a quantum bit, or qubit, works. While a classical computing bit is either 1 or 0, a qubit can be a blend of both called superposition. Patek Philippe’s 2026 releases 🚨🚨 a masterclass in horological authority but somehow making it feel effortless.

A 100-piece Nautilus 50th anniversary desk clock in white gold sets the tone, paired with the return of a midsize Nautilus (ref. 5810/11P, limited to 2,000). Then it escalates, a platinum Cubitus Perpetual Calendar Skeleton, the Celestial Sunrise & Sunset, a rose gold automaton, and several pieces that quietly remind everyone who’s still in charge.

#patekhilippe #2026 #watchcollector #patekworld
#swisswatches

📍 Patek Philippe marks 50 years of the Nautilus with a restrained but deliberate release: two oversized “Jumbo” interpretations and a refined midsize revival, plus a desk clock for good measure.

Leading the narrative is the return to purity. The new 41mm ref. 5810/1G models strip things back to the essence, no date, no center seconds, echoing the original 3700 proportions in a modern white gold execution at just 6.9mm thick.

More intriguing, though, is the ref. 5610/1P. A quiet nod to the 3800 lineage, it comes in a 38mm platinum case with a diamond set into the 9 o’clock ear. Again, no date, no seconds, just a clean dial with blue PVD sunburst finish, horizontal embossing, and white gold markers with lume.

Less noise, more intent. Exactly how an anniversary should be handled.

What does it actually look like to teach a robot how to work?
At this factory in India, workers wear head cameras as they stitch and assemble, recording every motion from their own perspective. 

Many of them are wearing head cameras that capture tasks from their point of view, creating real footage of how hands move, adjust, and assemble in a live production environment.

That footage becomes the raw material for AI training, helping robots understand the nuances of real human movement, not just rehearsed routines.

The future of automation is being built on the factory floor.

The sky, measured to perfection.
 
The Excalibur Biretrograde Perpetual Calendar distils the most demanding traditions of Geneva watchmaking into 40 mm of pink gold. At the heart of this piece lies the retrograde, the very first signature of the Maison, co-patented by the founder himself in 1989. Its perpetual calendar accounts for months of 28, 30 and 31 days without correction, expressed through a biretrograde display that brings mechanical poetry to the reading of time.
 
An astronomical moonphase, accurate to within one day over 122 years, completes this celestial portrait. Open-worked and structured across nine layers, the dial in Astral Blue transforms these classical complications into something resolutely present.
 
Calibre RD850. Poinçon de Genève certified.
 
Ref. RDDBEX1178

The Bugatti Tourbillon Sapphire Crystal by @jacobandco represents the pinnacle of hypercar-inspired watchmaking, crafted entirely from transparent sapphire crystal. Building on earlier versions, this model elevates exclusivity and design by revealing every intricate detail of the watch from all angles, including its Bugatti-inspired elements like the V16 engine automaton. The 54 x 44 mm case, carved from solid sapphire, is exceptionally difficult to produce, requiring over 800 hours of precise machining due to the material’s extreme hardness. Only two unique pieces exist, each showcasing bold colors and the brand’s signature design language.

Beyond its striking appearance, the watch integrates automotive engineering concepts into its movement and display. The Caliber JCAM55 mirrors a car engine’s architecture, featuring a 30-second tourbillon and a functioning V16 engine automaton with 16 moving pistons. The dial is inspired by a car dashboard, with retrograde hour and minute hands and indicators for both movement and automaton power reserves. The automaton can perform multiple animation sequences, bringing the miniature engine to life. Priced at $1.2 million, the timepiece embodies a seamless fusion of high watchmaking and automotive innovation.

#jacobandco #bugatti #tourbillon #sapphire #2026

A Glashütte Original is so much more than the sum of its parts! Every four weeks, we invite you to #ManufactorySunday and highlight different facets of our watchmaking art.

Today we’re taking a closer look at the Calibre 36. 

This automatic movement was designed from the ground up in-house some years ago. We pulled out all the stops when setting the requirements for this base calibre: it was to not only function reliably, but also deliver the highest precision, a long running time and robustness suitable for everyday use – not to mention looking absolutely stunning. 

To combine all these qualities within the smallest of spaces, our design team came up with some ingenious solutions. 

For the first time at Glashütte Original, a silicon balance spring was used: this high-tech material not only makes the wafer-thin balance spring resistant to magnetic fields and temperature fluctuations, but also allows it to oscillate with exceptional isochronism. To enhance shock resistance, the movement was secured in the case using a bayonet mount, similar to that of a camera lens. Thanks to this and many other ingenious measures, the Calibre 36 has set new standards. 

It is now available in several versions with various complications and powers a new generation of Glashütte Originals with precision, robustness and beauty.

#GlashütteOriginal #GlashuetteOriginal #FineWatchmaking #GermanWatches #WatchMovement

I edited this in @lightroom on Desktop and was able to bring it back to something that feels way closer to what it was like being there. 

Shaping the light, cleaning up the grain, and turning a split second underwater into something I can actually hold onto.

That’s what I love about it. Being able to take a moment like this and turn it into something more than just a photo.

Meet the next filmmaker in Beyond the Loop @jidenna 

Grammy-nominated musician, writer and actor, best known for his double platinum hit “Classic Man” and albums The Chief and 85 to Africa. Born to a Nigerian father and raised between continents, he’s always told stories that bridge cultures and defy genre.

Now he’s channelling that same creativity into AI filmmaking. And his take on it is worth paying attention to: Jidenna sees AI as the modern evolution of Hip-Hop sampling. Not a trend. A through-line. The same creative DNA that built a culture out of borrowed sounds, now building worlds out of generated frames.

His AI film catalogue, what he calls “random acts of flyness,” includes False Idols (2025), Jungle Fever (2024), and the micro-series Oga City.

In this week's Research Spotlight, Professor Victoria Ivashina and her coauthor found that rising borrowing costs significantly reduce firms' credit line drawdowns.

Lower rates support banks through deposits but encourage precautionary withdrawals, while higher rates reduce credit line use but create other vulnerabilities.

Jacob & Co. introduces a world first: The Godfather II, the only double melody musical timepiece. @jacobandco 

Jacob & Co. expands its popular Godfather-inspired collection with The Godfather II, a highly complex musical watch that plays two iconic melodies—“The Godfather’s Waltz” and “The Godfather Love Theme.” Designed in a curved, Art Deco-style case, the timepiece combines refined aesthetics with advanced engineering, including a flying tourbillon, dual power reserve indicators, and a unique melody selector that lets the wearer switch between songs. Limited to 74 pieces, the watch pays tribute to the 1974 release of The Godfather Part II while showcasing improved sound quality and a more compact design.

At the heart of caliber JCAM62 is an intricate music box system built around a comb and cylinder. The comb’s18 steel teeth produce different notes depending on their length, while a pin-studded brass cylinder activates them in sequence.A lateral shift of less than a millimeter, controlled by the melody selector, changes which pins engage the comb, allowing the watch to alternate between the two compositions.

Its design features references to the film, such as a portrait of Don Corleone, noir-inspired details, and symbolic engravings, while the intricate music box mechanism drives its signature melodies. For founder Jacob Arabo, the watch reflects both his admiration for the film and his own journey as an immigrant, making it not just a luxury timepiece but a deeply personal and artistic creation.

#jacobandco #tourbillon #godfather #doncorleone #corleone

The event at Gas South Arena in Duluth, Georgia, drew a crowd of 40 HBS alumni and guests, as well as members of the Atlanta Business School Alliance and local supporters of the Vibe. Following the discussion, attendees were invited to attend a match between the Atlanta Vibe and the Grand Rapids Rise.

“We wanted to bring women together around a story that’s unfolding in real time,” says Lisa Gunther (MBA 2000), one of the event’s organizers. “The HBSCA’s Women’s SIG is focused on elevating female leadership, whether that’s in boardrooms, startups, or entirely new arenas like women’s professional sports."

After launching successful cloud storage company Wasabi Technologies, founder and CEO David Friend was ready to scale the venture rapidly. The company had focused primarily on direct sales, but an opportunity to pivot toward channel sales was on the horizon. Wasabi’s major competitors like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft all sold their cloud storage products through multiple channels, and Friend feared that direct sales could never provide the momentum Wasabi Technologies needed to compete. However, making this pivot would mean changing its sales, marketing, and staffing strategies dramatically, and effectively veering the company away from its already successful course. 

On this episode of Cold Call, Senior Lecturer Lou Shipley joins Brian Kenny to discuss the case “Wasabi Technologies” and the questions Friend wrestled with:

▫️ Was channel sales the right play for the burgeoning cloud storage provider? 
▫️ If so, how should it best be implemented? 

They also explore ideas connected to Shipley’s new book, Unlikely Entrepreneurs.

Tune in at the link in bio.

Yesterday, our family sat on the couch, glued to the TV, brimming with anticipation as the countdown clock ticked 3… 2… 1… 

And then… LIFTOFF! 🌎 😮🚀

What is it about a space launch that gives us all collective goosebumps?

As we watched that extraordinary rocket, @nasa’s Artemis II, pierce the sky, we ran outside together, tears in our eyes, waving and cheering on these heroes as they began their 10-day, 250,000-mile journey around the Moon.

For the first time in more 50 years, humanity is returning to the Moon, not just to visit, but to circle it, together, in shared wonder.

Just before lift-off, the crew was asked if they were ready 🇺🇸👩‍🚀🇨🇦

“We are going for our families.”
“We are going for our teammates.”
“We are going for ALL HUMANITY.”

That’s what got me.

A courageous group of humans, some representing historic firsts, choosing to do something extraordinary not for themselves, but for all of us.

Watching this with our daughter… pure awe.

THIS IS HUMANITY AT ITS BEST.

Bold. 
Innovative. 
United. 
Driven by something greater than ourselves.

To Reid, Victor, Christina, and Jeremy, God bless you! 

What did you feel watching the launch? We’d love to hear!

A look back at one of our favorite projects and recipient of the AIA Honor Award — our mid-century pavilion in Pasadena’s San Rafael Heights.

We reimagined the home as a treehouse oasis, staying true to the neighborhood’s legacy of finely crafted mid-century modernism and a design tradition shaped by the West Coast’s climate and cultural hybridity.

Vacant and derelict for fourteen years, the house still revealed its original intent: a simple pavilion on pilotis, suspended within a serene garden and mature canopy. Its deep eaves and post-and-beam structure echo the language of postwar contemporaries like Buff & Hensman and Vladimir Ossipoff — architects who fused Japanese aesthetics, European modernism, and Arts and Crafts into a distinctly Californian vernacular.

The renovation is a contemporary homage to that lineage, defined by strong indoor-outdoor connections and a careful orchestration of light, air, and shadow.

This is a home designed to settle gracefully into its longevity, extending its Southern California context for years to come.

In their 2007 book Beyond HR: The New Science of Human Capital, John W. Boudreau and Peter M. Ramstad argue that HR needs to develop a decision science comparable to what finance built around ROI and marketing built around customer value. The authors documented implementations at Disney, Corning, and other organizations that had connected talent investments to strategic outcomes. Nearly two decades later, Severson, now an executive coach, uses their book to educate future CHROs and told me that such examples remain exceptions rather than standard practice.

As former Levi Strauss & Co. CHRO Tracy Layney put it to me, HR leaders should be held accountable for people outcomes with the same rigor “as you would around financial rigor, around customer rigor, around all your marketing metrics.”

Click on the link in our bio to read the full article

Adapted from "An AI Reckoning for HR: Transform or Fade Away" by Brian Elliott.

In the silent geometry of Tresigallo, known as “the metaphysical city”, architecture becomes an exercise in order, proportion, and light.

Redesigned in the 1930s as a rationalist ideal city, Tresigallo is defined by pure volumes, axial perspectives, and suspended atmospheres: squares framed by clean façades, pastel tones, and rigorous symmetry that evoke a dreamlike, almost timeless dimension.

Ann Murray Dunning, co-founder of @senoraeraofficial, didn’t begin her entrepreneurial journey feeling like the expert in the room. In the early days of building her first brand, Vamigas, she questioned whether she had enough experience or whether people would notice she was still learning.

So she leaned in.

Ann studied the industry, took classes, earned certifications, and learned everything she could about ingredients, marketing, and storytelling. Little by little, that learning turned into confidence.

That confidence carried forward.

The community her and her co-founder Christina Kelmon built through Vamigas eventually evolved into Señora Era 🌿 a home and lifestyle brand, newsletter, and book celebrating cozy living and cultural warmth inspired by Latin America.

And when it came to growing that community? She treated content like a creative experiment.

By testing Reels (especially Trial Reels) Ann discovered new amigas who connected with the message and helped expand the Señora Era movement.

Sometimes authority isn’t about having all the answers.

It’s about being willing to learn and showing up anyway.

AI answers your questions in seconds, but behind that speed is something called inference—the compute-intensive process where trained models generate responses.

Step 1: Tokenization (5-10 milliseconds) Faster than the blink of an eye (100-150 milliseconds) your question gets broken down into digestible pieces.

Step 2: Prefill (10-100 milliseconds) Still faster than a single eyeblink, the model is processing your full prompt and building something called a key-value cache—essentially a reference guide it will use to generate your answer.

Step 3: Decode (100-1,000+ milliseconds) About as long as it takes you to snap your fingers (roughly 150 milliseconds), the model constructs your entire answer, one piece at a time.

Step 4: Detokenization (final milliseconds) Before you can say “thank you” (about 600 milliseconds) those tokens are being converted back into readable text and streaming to your screen.

Link in bio

Why is @pelhamvancooten, Data Engineer, still excited to be at Meta after 5+ years? It comes down to two things: the incredibly smart people he gets to work with every day and the opportunity to keep growing. 👨‍💻 

Pelham joined as an intern because he loved Instagram — he was a total power user who wanted to work on something he cared about. But he stayed because of the culture of collaboration and meaningful growth.

From day one, Pelham was trusted with real projects. Not just shadowing or busy work, but actual contributions that mattered. And that encouraged him to grow fast.

When we last connected with Jesse Íñiguez of @backoftheyardscoffeeco, his coffeehouse was already more than just a coffee shop — it was a community anchor. Now, the team is building on that foundation in a big way.

This past year marked a major milestone — the opening of a new location inside the historic Goldblatt’s building on the southwest side of Chicago. “Beyond being a coffeehouse, this space represents resilience, community, and connection,” Jesse shared. It’s a full-circle moment for the brand, rooted in the same neighborhood where the idea first began.

Since opening their doors, the space has become a hub for intentional community gatherings — Senior Mondays, community Pilates, brunch with live music, and the return of their beloved Story Time series in partnership with the local library. Whether it’s wellness or joy or a warm cup of coffee, the coffeehouse is delivering it with care.

The story doesn’t stop in Chicago.

Back of the Yards Coffee is set to open two new coffeehouses — one in Pilsen, Chicago, and another all the way in Guadalajara, Mexico, marking their first international expansion. Both spaces will continue to celebrate Latin American culture and connection, through exceptional coffee and welcoming community energy.

And as they grow, social media continues to be one of their strongest tools.

“Reels have been a great asset for us, allowing us to reach new audiences and show more people how we bring communities together through coffee,” Jesse said. The brand also leaned into Instagram’s collaboration feature to partner with local businesses and tap into shared audiences — growing their following by 50% over the past year.

For Jesse, the key to community-building through content is simple: “Consistency and authenticity are everything. Showing up regularly and sharing genuine stories is how you grow organically.”

☕️ From Chicago to Guadalajara, Back of the Yards Coffee is just getting started.

📲 Follow @backoftheyardscoffeeco to stay tapped in with every new blend, new location, and new reason to gather.

▶️ Generate a 3-minute track, then scrub through to find the best cut.
▶️ Add that audio to your clip to create a polished soundtrack.

In this week’s Stability Seconds, we’re showing you how to use prompt weighting, a technique that helps you control which parts of your prompt have more or less influence on the final image. It’s a fast way to steer your image toward your desired output without rewriting your prompt.

Here’s how to do it:

1️⃣ Emphasize elements: Put parentheses around the part of your prompt you want to focus on, then add a colon and a number. For example, (trench coat:1.5) tells the model to emphasize the trench coat. The higher the number, the more it stands out.

2️⃣ De-emphasize elements: Put parentheses around the part of your prompt you want to make less prominent, then add a colon and a number below one. For example, (background:0.5). The lower the number, the less it stands out.

You can try prompt weighting with Stable Image Ultra on our website's learning hub.

So much of what we do centers around seeing possibilities. At this 1920’s Spanish Revival house in the hills of Silver Lake, a piece of this potential was an extensive addition.

It has become faddish over the last fifty years to understand additions as opportunities for juxtaposition, for compositional dialogues – sometimes very stark in their relationship.

Our approach for this project was grounded in an understanding of the house’s lack of integrity, its lack of completeness, but also in potent remnants of its original conception – beautiful stucco walls and a period tile roof with charming bird mouthed rafter tails – key tenants of an architectural language that is synonymous with Los Angeles.

Sadly, the 1200 sq/ft house had been quickly renovated by the last owner – new vinyl windows, big box store tile, the works – most of the original details lost. This house, the artifact, lacked the force and quality to command an architectural dialogue. Instead, the new 1500 sq/ft wing was seen as a grafting on to the old – an extension of it, completing the original idea in our time, while satisfying the needs of the present: a large kitchen – a place to gather and to entertain, proper service spaces and storage, a principal suite, and closet after closet. 

Photographs by Virtually Here Studios

文長，但阿姨真心希望大家能耐心看完並分享給身邊的親朋好友們，或許可以避免更多人被詐騙🙏

近期攔阻了一些詐騙事件，花費了我不少時間與精力，想在此與大家分享幾個案例，好讓大家能把這案例分享給身邊的親朋好友，避免遭受詐騙或淪為詐騙集團的一員而不自知！

案例ㄧ
客戶一早到銀行辦理匯款，同仁詢問是什麼款項？收款人是否認識？
客戶表示收款人他很熟、是要裝潢的款項，並且一直催促同仁趕緊匯款。
阿姨身上有裝詐騙雷達，總覺得什麼地方怪怪的🤔於是故意跟客戶說這帳號少一碼，你打電話給對方確認一下，不然匯不出去。
這時客戶突然語塞表示沒有對方的電話😏
阿姨：ㄚ你不是說收款人你很熟？而且是幫你裝潢的廠商，怎麼會沒有聯絡電話？
這時客戶整個人像當機一樣，低頭不語。
阿姨請同仁通知警察來行協助，警察來時這客戶一句話仍都不肯說，阿姨不斷的跟他說是不是有什麼困難讓你一直不願透露，他才説因為檢察官有警告他不能說，不然會被通輯、會被關，這時客戶從身上拿出一張法院的傳票，警察一看就知道這是偽造的假傳票，請客戶不要擔心，更不要匯款！這時客戶才鬆了一口氣，不斷咳嗽與冒汗，趕緊為他遞上面紙，他脫下口罩那ㄧ剎那，阿姨差點掉下眼淚，原來他口腔癌，還拖著不適的身體趕來銀行匯款給詐騙集團，詐騙集團真的是王八蛋🤬
還好40萬守住了🙏（大家不要覺得怎麼還會有人相信檢察官這種戲碼，鄉下地方有些年長或是弱勢族群對於現今社會時事資訊不足，他們真的非常單純與善良，根本不知道詐騙集團的手法）。

案例二
最近詐騙最多的案例，大多是fb 、line等社群網站跳出訊息並強力推薦有哪一檔股票或投資平台可以讓你獲利爆棚，有興趣者加line而且不收費，加入line群組後，可想而知，這群組設計了非常完美的圖表📈，看了讓你心癢癢，你的錢已經迫不及待的想匯出投資，詐騙集團為了讓你投入更多資金，有的故意匯了頗高的利息報酬到你的帳戶，讓你先嚐嚐甜頭，以為真的壓對寶，於是你會愈投愈多，最後當你想提領投資的錢時，對方已封鎖你！一毛錢也拿不回來！
拜託🙏～～不要再相信網路、虛擬的愛情與投資！

案例三
有位年近60的大姐要匯款至國外，詢問她匯款的對象及用途，她一下說對方是她老公、一下又是男朋友，匯錢是打算要借錢給他，（其實我很清楚這是個愛情詐騙手法），於是我請她打個電話給對方，我想跟他聊一下，這位大姐卻說她男朋友是美國調查局的高級官員，現在在艦艇上出任務無法接電話，我詢問她有見過對方嗎，她說僅透過line的訊息來交談，非常相信對方對她的感情，堅持要匯款給對方，完全不接受我拒絕她匯款的理由（因為我粉碎了她的愛情夢），最後只好請警察大人來行協助，這位大姐最後氣噗噗的離開～

案例四
又到了學生畢業的季節，相對的詐騙集團也伺機而動，請提醒身邊剛畢業找工作或工讀的孩子們，別莫名淪為詐騙集團的一員。
當有公司用各種名義要你交出存摺及提款卡，千萬要勇敢說不，並轉頭離開那間公司。因為若把存摺及提款卡交給了對方，這帳戶極有可能被詐騙集團給利用。看了許多單純的孩子因此要上警局說明解釋，真的心有餘而力不足。

另外有網友私訊阿姨希望我能提醒目前詐騙手法～裸聊～
許多年輕學生在交友網站結識網友，詐騙集團常利用一般人喜歡正妹美女的心態，透過視訊裸聊，藉機擷取當事人不雅影片畫面，並抓住被害人擔心曝光的心理來詐騙。裸聊時間通常僅約1分鐘，因詐騙集團目的只在擷取到被害人的不雅畫面，且事實上被害人所看到對方的畫面，也可能並非真實，只是網路上其他網友的裸露影片遭詐騙集團所用的假視訊；接著網友會聲稱自己是直播主，以要去直播為由，邀請被害人參與，但是所傳送的QR Code實際上是夾帶病毒的木馬程式，被害人一旦下載安裝，手機裡的通訊錄等個資就會全部被詐騙集團取得，再依此要求被害人購買遊戲點數，才能刪除影片跟通訊錄，這就是此種詐騙手法的標準套路。

提醒大家切莫過度依賴、信賴網路交友，同時應避免因為好奇、新鮮、刺激，而與不認識的網友進行裸露身體的視訊對話，聊天中如對方以任何理由傳送不明連結、QR Code要求下載、安裝、輸入個人手機號碼或邀請碼等，也請勿輕易點選輸入，以免個資外洩，成為詐騙集團予取予求的籌碼

溫馨提醒～
社群網站總是會有人使用很帥很美的照片來吸引你加好友，然後上傳的照片千篇一律都是超跑、遊艇、名錶、飛機、美酒、美食，有些是近期才申請帳號並連續發文，有些買粉絲來製造人氣假象，讓你放下戒心，鎖定目標後就不斷私訊你，對你述說人生的苦，讓你產生憐憫，然後噓寒問暖，讓你慢慢的陷入了這網路愛情陷阱！
（看到這類交友邀請直接封鎖）
 
看到這裡還沒有給阿姨一個讚或分享，沒道理ㄚ🤣

大家若也有遇到詐騙集團的經驗，請不吝分享一下，好讓大家能更加了解詐騙手法🙏
